{"key_event":"Organized labor rises amid nitrate shock","reasoning":"Civil society strengthened as FOCh, the Partido Obrero Socialista, worker newspapers, strikes, and Alessandri-style mass politics broadened mobilization beyond oligarchic networks, though suffrage and repression still limited popular constraint. State power remained strong in coercion and administration and gained modest labor-regulatory reach, but parliamentary fragmentation and World War I nitrate revenue instability reduced autonomous fiscal-executive capacity.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":7.6,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":8.1}
